frameworks.name: somatic doctrine of tDCS polarity
frameworks.one_line: anodal increases cortical excitability while cathodal decreases it

frameworks.name: GABAergic/glutamatergic excitation-inhibition balance
frameworks.one_line: neurotransmitter concentrations index cortical excitability state

core_question: Does anodal/cathodal tDCS over auditory cortex modulate local GABA and glutamate concentrations measured by 7T MR-spectroscopy?
key_assumptions: MRS-measured GABA and glutamate index cortical excitation-inhibition balance
tDCS effects on neurotransmitters are detectable within and immediately after stimulation
auditory cortex behaves as a canonical sensory cortex with respect to tDCS polarity
manipulations: tDCS polarity over auditory cortex (anodal vs cathodal)
key_findings: anodal tDCS reduced GABA in targeted auditory cortex
anodal tDCS produced no significant glutamate change
cathodal tDCS produced the opposite GABA pattern
interpretations: directional GABA modulation by polarity matches the somatic-doctrine prediction in sensory cortex
